meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: Solve 'No Project Ideas' with Client Workflows
content.seo_description: Stop hunting for ideas. Learn to build high-converting client workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate your SaaS and generate revenue fast.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start solving 'bottlenecks.' By mapping client workflows, you identify high-friction manual processes ripe for automation. Build an MVP that solves one specific, expensive pain point for a defined niche. Revenue is the ultimate validation; build what pays, not what you think is cool.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. Every day you spend 'brainstorming' is a day your competitors are shipping, iterating, and capturing market share. You aren't suffering from a lack of creativity; you are suffering from a lack of observation. The most profitable SaaS products aren't born from genius epiphanies—they are born from the friction points in existing professional workflows.

To build a product people will pay for, you must stop being an 'idea guy' and start being a 'workflow architect.' By analyzing how businesses move data and make decisions, you uncover the gaps where manual labor currently drains their profit. This guide forces you to stop guessing and start building, using modern AI tooling to convert observations into a revenue-generating MVP.

steps.title: Identify the Revenue-Draining Workflow
steps.description: Find a niche industry that still relies on spreadsheets or manual data entry. Interview a practitioner to map their 'Current State' process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this interview transcript [PASTE TRANSCRIPT]. Identify three distinct tasks that take over 2 hours per day and involve manual data movement between apps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a markdown table comparing manual effort vs potential automation ROI for these three identified tasks.'

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Select the single most expensive pain point. Strip away all features that don't directly solve that one specific bottleneck.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Given the workflow bottleneck [DESCRIBE], design a minimal feature set for a SaaS MVP that solves this in under 10 clicks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a user story map for this MVP focusing exclusively on the core workflow path.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Data Architecture
steps.description: Structure your database schema to handle the specific workflow inputs identified in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS that manages [WORKFLOW]. Include tables for users, workflow_instances, and audit_logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create migration files for this schema and add basic CRUD endpoints using FastApi.'

steps.title: Build the Logic Engine
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold the core business logic that transforms raw inputs into the desired output.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the business logic handler for [WORKFLOW_STEP]. Ensure it includes error handling for API timeouts and data validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core transformation logic and write unit tests for the edge cases.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your MVP to a staging environment and get it in front of a real user immediately for feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for this application, including environment variables, database indexing, and essential security headers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure a deployment script that pushes this to a VPS and sets up basic monitoring.'

pro_tips: Always charge for the MVP; if they won't pay $50 for a beta, they won't pay $500 for the full product.
Focus on 'Time-to-Value'—your UI should guide the user to the result in under 60 seconds.
If you can't describe the value proposition in one sentence, your workflow is too complex and needs further pruning.
Use Claude Code to write your integration tests first; it forces you to define the expected output before writing the implementation code.
Avoid building custom dashboards until the user has proven they use the primary workflow tool daily.
resources: The Mom Test (Book on Customer Discovery): https://www.momtestbook.com
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, reach out to 10 potential users in your niche. Don't sell; ask them to use the tool in exchange for a lifetime discount. Iterate based on their actual usage data, not their feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum and start solving real problems that businesses will pay to fix. Your next million-dollar idea is hidden in a spreadsheet somewhere. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
